what_it_does: A posted noticeboard comment by Tim in the dragnet-development channel that replies to item-47cb5563, acknowledging UX problems with his proposed paragraph gestures and clarifying that his earlier description was an experiential idea rather than a specification.
observations: Author 'tim' posted a comment linked as a comment_on board://item-47cb5563.
The body quotes a critique about iOS native long-press and vertical-drag behaviors conflicting with proposed paragraph selection gestures.
Tim states he is not tied to the specific mechanisms and that his initial description was meant to convey an experience, not a specification.
He welcomes findings that invalidate the idea and asks for options that might work instead.
He notes he has not read the rest of the page yet, so he does not know if suggestions are already present.
Metadata shows created and updated timestamps identical with a single 'filed' history entry.
standouts: Explicit reframing of the prior description as non-binding experiential ideas rather than implementation specs.
Recognition that failing to suppress native iOS gestures would be a total failure of the core interaction, not a polish issue.
Direct request for collaborators to propose workable alternatives after invalidating the current sketch.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a markdown body with a quoted prior paragraph followed by an inline response.
Links to another board item via kind: commented_on.
concerns: The response is written before reading the rest of the page, so it may duplicate or miss suggestions already present.
No concrete alternatives or next steps are proposed within this file.
notes: Tim's reply: the gestures are disposable, the experience goal is not, and he wants feasible alternatives.
questions: What is the full content of the parent item board://item-47cb5563?
What specific interaction is being designed (e.g., the 'level-picker')?
What suggestions or alternatives, if any, appear later in the thread?
What is the scope and current state of the dragnet-development project?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is a comment/reply; its content matches that purpose by responding to a prior note.
apparent_themes: Non-binding ideation as a starting point
intention_signals: Tim is trying to preserve the user-experience intent behind his sketch while inviting collaborators to discard the specific gestures and propose technically viable replacements.
novelty: An unusually candid author note that explicitly invites invalidation of the proposed mechanism and separates personal experience description from product specification.
contribution: Records the author's intent that the underlying experience, not the specific gestures, is the design target; captures the iOS native gesture conflict as a core blocker; and documents a request for alternative interaction approaches.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard comment by Tim acknowledging that proposed paragraph-level long-press and vertical-drag gestures conflict with native iOS behaviors, clarifying that his earlier description was an experiential idea rather than a specification, and asking for workable alternatives.
intention_for_embedding: To respond to UX critique, decouple the underlying experience goal from the proposed gestures, and solicit feasible interaction options.
